project.name: Craft
project.summary: High-performance TypeScript immutable state update package with patches, composition helpers, Map/Set support, docs, tests, and benchmarks.
goals: Provide the public @sylphx/craft package exports and debug export.
Own immutable update behavior, draft/finalize helpers, patches, composition utilities, Map/Set support, benchmarks, tests, and docs.
Keep package performance claims, bundle claims, release notes, and generated API docs backed by current evidence.
nonGoals: Own application-specific state models, UI framework bindings, persistence, synchronization, or customer business workflows.
Own enterprise doctrine, package release infrastructure, or consuming application delivery policy.
Own downstream app performance budgets beyond the package API and benchmark surface.

owns.name: craft-package-api
owns.description: The @sylphx/craft package exports, debug export, immutable update semantics, patch semantics, composition helpers, Map/Set support, and package metadata.

owns.name: craft-docs-tests-benchmarks
owns.description: Repository README, docs site content, release notes, tests, benchmarks, bundle/performance claims, and package examples.

doesNotOwn: Application-specific state models, UI framework bindings, persistence, synchronization, server cache policy, or customer business workflows.
Package release infrastructure owned by reusable SylphxAI workflows or consuming application delivery policy.
Enterprise engineering doctrine and manifest schema owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema and engineering doctrine

forbiddenCouplings: Do not add application-specific state models, UI framework adapters, persistence, or customer workflow assumptions to the package core.
Do not make consumers depend on internal source files, benchmark internals, or documentation implementation details instead of package exports.
Do not fork doctrine standards into this repository.

delivery.deployPath: Main-branch Release delegates to SylphxAI/.github reusable release workflow for package publication; no PR CI workflow is present in .github/workflows at this baseline.
delivery.productionProof: Passing biome check, typecheck, tests, build, relevant benchmarks for performance changes, successful release workflow, and package-registry/readme readback for published versions.

gaps.description: The repository has release automation but no PR/merge_group CI workflow recorded at this baseline.
README includes performance, bundle-size, and coverage claims; keep benchmarks and generated docs fresh before treating those claims as production proof.
gaps.target: next package performance or docs release

gaps.description: Published package versions are forward-only for consumers; add bad-release and registry recovery guidance before full adoption.
